intro: Sphenogyne scariosa, also known as Sphenogyne, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to Australia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin issues. The bioactive constituents of Sphenogyne scariosa include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Sphenogyne scariosa is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, sore throats, and skin conditions.
